title: pyrus communis subsp. mariana
intro: Pyrus communis subsp. mariana, also known as the wild service tree, is a subspecies of the service tree (Pyrus communis) native to Europe. The bark and wood of the wild service tree have been traditionally used for their medicinal properties, particularly in treating digestive issues, diarrhea, and skin conditions. The plant contains a range of bioactive compounds, including flavonoids and phenolic acids, which provide various health benefits, including antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial effects. The wild service tree is often used in herbal preparations, such as tinctures, infusions, and decoctions, and is also used to make a sweet, edible fruit that is rich in vitamins and minerals.
